_meta.note: Synthetic issue tracker. Each ticket's id IS its source_id.

tickets.title: Add order tagging feature
tickets.description: Let merchants tag orders (e.g. 'gift', 'priority'). Requires a new order_tags table and an index on orders(tag_id) for filtering. Targeted for the v4.2.0 release.

comments.author: Marcus Chen
comments.text: Schema done. Adding an index on orders(tag_id) so the tag filter query is fast.

comments.author: Sam Okoro
comments.text: Heads up, orders is a huge table. Did you check the migration guidelines?

comments.author: Marcus Chen
comments.text: It's a quick index, should be fine. Merging for v4.2.0.

tickets.title: INCIDENT: v4.2.0 checkout outage + release delay
tickets.description: During the v4.2.0 production deploy on 2026-06-03, checkout error rate spiked to ~40% and the deploy was rolled back. Release slipped from June 3 to June 6.
tickets.root_cause: The v4.2.0 deploy ran a migration (commit a3f9c2, from NW-1042) that created an index on the orders table with a plain CREATE INDEX (non-concurrent). This took an exclusive lock on the orders table for ~6 minutes, blocking all checkout writes. This violated the Database Migration Guidelines, which require CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on large tables.
action_items: Recreate the index with CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY (commit b7e1d4).
Add a CI lint check that fails any plain CREATE INDEX on tables in the large-table list.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.text: Rolled back v4.2.0. orders table was locked during the deploy migration. Checkout writes were blocked.

comments.author: Aisha Khan
comments.text: Confirmed: the tag index migration used plain CREATE INDEX. Our guidelines require CONCURRENTLY on orders. This is the root cause.

comments.author: Marcus Chen
comments.text: Fixed in b7e1d4 with CONCURRENTLY. Sorry — I underestimated the lock on a table that size.

tickets.title: Enhanced fraud detection on payments
tickets.description: Call the fraud-check service before authorizing a payment to score risk. Improves fraud catch rate.

comments.author: Elena Rodriguez
comments.text: Wiring the fraud-check call into the authorize() path. Shipping with v4.2.x.

tickets.title: INCIDENT: payments-gateway p99 latency spike
tickets.description: On 2026-06-10 payments-gateway p99 latency spiked from ~250ms to ~8s. Checkout conversions dropped. No errors, just slowness.
tickets.root_cause: Commit c4d8e1 (from NW-1120) added a SYNCHRONOUS, un-timed call to the fraud-check service inside the payment authorization hot path. fraud-check became slow under afternoon peak load, and because the call had no timeout and no fallback, every payment authorization blocked on it. This violated the Service Call Guidelines (hot-path calls must be async or have a <200ms timeout + fallback).
action_items: Make the fraud-check call async with a 150ms timeout and a fail-open fallback (commit d9a2f7).

comments.author: Elena Rodriguez
comments.text: p99 8s on payments. Traced it to the new fraud-check call — it's synchronous and has no timeout.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.text: fraud-check is slow under load. We blocked the whole auth path on a non-critical service. That's exactly what the hot-path guideline warns against.

tickets.title: Make fraud-check call async (timeout + fallback)
tickets.description: Move the fraud-check call off the hot path: 150ms timeout, fail-open fallback, async risk review. Resolves NW-1130.

tickets.title: Flaky integration tests on checkout-svc
tickets.description: checkout-svc integration tests fail ~1 in 4 runs due to a race condition in the test harness (order created before inventory mock is ready). Blocks tagging a clean release candidate.

comments.author: Marcus Chen
comments.text: It's a test-harness race, not a product bug. Adding a readiness wait. ETA a couple days.

tickets.title: Release v4.3.0
tickets.description: Coordinating ticket for the v4.3.0 release, targeted 2026-06-17.

comments.author: Tom Becker
comments.text: v4.3.0 is blocked on NW-1148 (flaky checkout integration tests). We will not tag an RC until CI is green. No other open blockers.
